name: RAPPstore — official
description: Public catalog of rapplications. Each rapplication is a converged single-file agent.py produced by the double-jump loop. Pull into your local brainstem's agents/ directory and it works. Optional .egg pack restores example workflow state.
install_protocol: drop the singleton .py file into your local brainstem's `agents/` dir. Then POST /api/swarm/deploy with a bundle that includes it, OR: simply hot-load it from disk if your brainstem watches the agents/ folder. The file is everything — no install script, no pip, no native deps.

rapplications.name: Twin Workshop
rapplications.summary: Workshop a personal twin in 60 minutes. Asks what you want to delegate, designs the agent in front of you, validates against your test input, hands you the .py. Runs everywhere a brainstem does.
rapplications.tagline: The full workshop loop, performed by the platform on itself, with the customer in the room.
rapplications.egg_note: Stateless per workshop session — no shipped state.

args.description: I want an agent that answers prospect emails the way I would.

rapplications.name: BookFactory
rapplications.summary: Five-persona content pipeline (Writer → Editor [3 specialists] → CEO [2 specialists] → Publisher → Reviewer). One call, one chapter back. Best for: engineering writeups, blog posts, briefing docs, narrative content under 1500 words.
rapplications.tagline: Source material in, publishable chapter out — 8 LLM calls under one POST.
rapplications.egg_note: No pre-populated state .egg shipped — BookFactory is stateless per call. Output artifacts are written to the workspace dir specified per call.

rapplications.name: BookFactory (with Kody+Molly demo state)
rapplications.summary: Five-persona content pipeline (Writer → Editor [3 specialists] → CEO [2 specialists] → Publisher → Reviewer). One call, one chapter back. Best for: engineering writeups, blog posts, briefing docs, narrative content under 1500 words. · This demo edition ships with a pre-packaged .egg containing the Kody+Molly book-factory workspaces from cycle 3, so you can unpack it with `bash rapp_swarm/twin-egg.sh unpack` and immediately see what a real run produces — source material, draft, edited prose, CEO note, final chapter, reviewer score.
rapplications.tagline: BookFactory + a worked Kody/Molly run packed as one .egg you can unpack and inspect.
rapplications.egg_note: Contains 6 twin workspaces (kody, molly, writer, editor, publisher, reviewer) plus the in-flight book-factory artifacts under .shared/. Unpack with `bash rapp_swarm/twin-egg.sh unpack bookfactoryagent-demo.egg --into ~/.rapp-twins`. Restoration is byte-identical and SHA-256-verified.
rapplications.install_after_unpack: Already-hatched swarms come with the .egg. Start any twin: bash rapp_swarm/twin-sim.sh start kody. Then call BookFactory through that twin's brainstem to see a complete worked example.

rapplications.name: Writer Persona
rapplications.summary: Senior nonfiction writer. Takes raw source material and returns chapter-style prose with code preserved verbatim.
rapplications.tagline: Senior nonfiction writer. Takes raw source material and returns chapter-style prose with code preserved verbatim.

rapplications.name: Publisher Persona
rapplications.summary: Indie-press publisher. Takes edited prose plus a CEO note and outputs publication-ready markdown with YAML frontmatter.
rapplications.tagline: Indie-press publisher. Takes edited prose plus a CEO note and outputs publication-ready markdown with YAML frontmatter.

rapplications.name: Reviewer Persona
rapplications.summary: Cold-read reviewer. Returns a 1-5 score, the strongest single line, and whether they would keep reading.
rapplications.tagline: Cold-read reviewer. Returns a 1-5 score, the strongest single line, and whether they would keep reading.

rapplications.name: Editor: Cut Weakest 20%
rapplications.summary: Editor specialist. Returns the same prose with the weakest 20% cut. Preserves fenced code blocks as evidence.
rapplications.tagline: Editor specialist. Returns the same prose with the weakest 20% cut. Preserves fenced code blocks as evidence.

rapplications.name: Editor: Factcheck
rapplications.summary: Editor specialist. Surfaces 2-5 claims that need a source and what kind of source would prove or disprove each.
rapplications.tagline: Editor specialist. Surfaces 2-5 claims that need a source and what kind of source would prove or disprove each.

rapplications.name: Editor: Voice Drift
rapplications.summary: Editor specialist. Flags voice drift — sentences that don't sound like the author's established voice.
rapplications.tagline: Editor specialist. Flags voice drift — sentences that don't sound like the author's established voice.

rapplications.name: Editor: Strip Scaffolding
rapplications.summary: Editor specialist. Removes ## Outline headers, TODO markers, and draft-state labels left by the writer.
rapplications.tagline: Editor specialist. Removes ## Outline headers, TODO markers, and draft-state labels left by the writer.

rapplications.name: Editor: Restructure
rapplications.summary: Editor specialist. Consolidates repetitive middle sections without rewriting the writer's voice.
rapplications.tagline: Editor specialist. Consolidates repetitive middle sections without rewriting the writer's voice.

rapplications.name: CEO: Decision
rapplications.summary: CEO specialist. Returns a one-line ship/edit/hold decision with the reasoning behind it.
rapplications.tagline: CEO specialist. Returns a one-line ship/edit/hold decision with the reasoning behind it.

rapplications.name: CEO: Partner Risk
rapplications.summary: CEO specialist. Lists 1-3 partner-conversation risks that would actually matter in a real meeting.
rapplications.tagline: CEO specialist. Lists 1-3 partner-conversation risks that would actually matter in a real meeting.

rapplications.name: Editor Persona (composite)
rapplications.summary: Editor persona. Composite of 5 specialists: strip → cut → restructure, plus factcheck + voicecheck for the editor's note.
rapplications.tagline: Editor persona. Composite of 5 specialists: strip → cut → restructure, plus factcheck + voicecheck for the editor's note.

rapplications.name: CEO Persona (composite)
rapplications.summary: CEO persona. Composite of 2 specialists: decision + partner-risk. Returns a strategic-message-discipline review.
rapplications.tagline: CEO persona. Composite of 2 specialists: decision + partner-risk. Returns a strategic-message-discipline review.

rapplications.name: BookFactory (multi-file source)
rapplications.summary: Five-persona content pipeline (Writer → Editor → CEO → Publisher → Reviewer) as a composite of 13 sibling agent.py files.
rapplications.tagline: Five-persona content pipeline (Writer → Editor → CEO → Publisher → Reviewer) as a composite of 13 sibling agent.py files.

rapplications.name: MomentFactory
rapplications.summary: Seven-persona moment-to-Drop pipeline (Sensorium → SignificanceFilter → HookWriter → BodyWriter → ChannelRouter → CardForger → SeedStamper). Turns any raw moment (commit, voice memo, web bookmark, agent run, location, conversation, decision, reading note) into a Rappterbook Drop with built-in significance filtering. The candidate engine for any feed-driven AI-agent platform.
rapplications.tagline: Any moment in. One Drop out — with the SignificanceFilter's veto built into the pipeline.
rapplications.egg_note: MomentFactory is stateless per call — no .egg shipped. Pair with a Rappterbook state .egg if you want a worked example.

args.source_type: code-commit | voice-memo | web-bookmark | agent-run | location | conversation | decision | reading-note

rapplications.name: Sensorium
rapplications.summary: Normalizes a raw moment (code commit, voice memo, bookmark, agent run, location, conversation, decision, reading note) into a structured JSON shape.
rapplications.tagline: Normalizes a raw moment (code commit, voice memo, bookmark, agent run, location, conversation, decision, reading note) into a structured JSON shape.

rapplications.name: Significance Filter
rapplications.summary: Refuses moments that don't compound. The platform's defining constraint, encoded as one persona with veto power. Default: ship=false.
rapplications.tagline: Refuses moments that don't compound. The platform's defining constraint, encoded as one persona with veto power. Default: ship=false.

rapplications.name: Hook Writer
rapplications.summary: Writes the one sentence that earns a tap on the feed. Single sentence. Concrete over abstract. No clickbait.
rapplications.tagline: Writes the one sentence that earns a tap on the feed. Single sentence. Concrete over abstract. No clickbait.

rapplications.name: Body Writer
rapplications.summary: Writes the 3-5 sentence body of a Drop. Expands the hook, never restates. At least one concrete detail from key_facts.
rapplications.tagline: Writes the 3-5 sentence body of a Drop. Expands the hook, never restates. At least one concrete detail from key_facts.

rapplications.name: Channel Router
rapplications.summary: Picks one Subrappter (r/builders, r/dreams, r/decisions, r/lessons, r/connections, etc.) for a Drop. Auto-classifies, no manual tagging.
rapplications.tagline: Picks one Subrappter (r/builders, r/dreams, r/decisions, r/lessons, r/connections, etc.) for a Drop. Auto-classifies, no manual tagging.

rapplications.name: Card Forger
rapplications.summary: Mints a RAR-compatible card (name + impact/novelty/compoundability stats + ability + lore + art_seed) from a Drop. Every Drop is a collectible.
rapplications.tagline: Mints a RAR-compatible card (name + impact/novelty/compoundability stats + ability + lore + art_seed) from a Drop. Every Drop is a collectible.

rapplications.name: Seed Stamper
rapplications.summary: Pure function (no LLM). Returns deterministic 64-bit seed + 7-word incantation from a 256-word wordlist. Speak the words → reconstruct the Drop offline.
rapplications.tagline: Pure function (no LLM). Returns deterministic 64-bit seed + 7-word incantation from a 256-word wordlist. Speak the words → reconstruct the Drop offline.

rapplications.name: MomentFactory (multi-file source)
rapplications.summary: Seven-persona moment-to-Drop pipeline as a composite of 8 sibling agent.py files. The candidate engine for any feed-driven AI-agent platform.
rapplications.tagline: Seven-persona moment-to-Drop pipeline as a composite of 8 sibling agent.py files. The candidate engine for any feed-driven AI-agent platform.

rapplications.name: MomentFactory (converged singleton)
rapplications.summary: Seven-persona moment-to-Drop pipeline collapsed into a single sacred file. Hatches alone with zero sibling deps. The candidate engine for Rappterbook.
rapplications.tagline: Seven-persona moment-to-Drop pipeline collapsed into a single sacred file. Hatches alone with zero sibling deps. The candidate engine for Rappterbook.

rapplications.name: ExecBrief
rapplications.summary: Five-stage executive brief pipeline (Scout → Analyst → Strategist → Writer → DeckForge) with live index-card progress. Each stage animates in the UI as it runs, then freezes into a report card with the brief and pitch deck as clickable artifacts. DeckForge is optional — requires @rapp/pitch_deck to be installed.
rapplications.tagline: Any topic in, one brief and a pitch deck out — 5 stages, one live index card.
rapplications.egg_note: ExecBrief is stateless per call — no .egg shipped. Output artifacts are written to the workspace dir. DeckForge stage calls @rapp/pitch_deck when installed; gracefully degrades to brief-only when not.

body.user_input: Create an executive brief about why Microsoft needs a unified agent sharing standard

rapplications.name: PitchDeck
rapplications.summary: Generates a polished HTML executive pitch deck from a topic and thesis. Output: a single self-contained HTML file with exec/rehearse modes, light/dark theme, keyboard/swipe nav, email-preview and CTA components.
rapplications.tagline: Topic in, boardroom-ready deck out — one self-contained HTML file.
rapplications.egg_note: PitchDeck writes decks to the pitches/ workspace dir under the brainstem. Each call produces one self-contained HTML file.

body.user_input: Generate a pitch deck for our internal agent-sharing proposal aimed at engineering leadership

rapplications.name: Binder
rapplications.summary: The brainstem's package manager. Browse the RAPPstore catalog, install or uninstall rapplications. Bootstrapped by start.sh on first launch so the brainstem itself ships clean.

rapplications.name: Swarms
rapplications.summary: Group your agents into named swarms (stack or converged) and toggle which swarms are active per turn. Surfaces as the swarm bar in the chat UI.

rapplications.name: Kanban
rapplications.summary: Task board you talk to. Create, move, and query tasks through any LLM.

rapplications.name: Webhook
rapplications.summary: Query incoming events from external systems through any LLM.

rapplications.name: Dashboard
rapplications.summary: Track and query metrics through conversation with any LLM.

rapplications.name: VibeBuilder
rapplications.summary: Build a complete rapplication (agent + service) from natural language.

senses.name: haiku
senses.summary: Translates the response into a 5/7/5 haiku. Same meaning, different mode.

senses.name: tldr
senses.summary: Compresses the response to a single load-bearing sentence.

senses.name: emoji
senses.summary: Translates the response into a 3-7 emoji narrative — a tone fingerprint.

senses.name: eli5
senses.summary: Re-explains the response so a five-year-old could follow it.

senses.name: headline
senses.summary: Compresses the response into a punchy news-ticker headline.

publish_your_own.summary: Author a rapplication using the multi-file source pattern (see blog/89). Run the double-jump loop until convergence (typically 1-3 cycles per content shape). Collapse to a singleton via tools/build-rapplication.py. Submit a PR adding an entry to rapp_store/index.json.

install_protocol_senses: Drop the *_sense.py file into your local brainstem at rapp_brainstem/senses/. Auto-discovered each chat request, no restart. Senses are TRANSLATIONS of the main response into different modes of expression (Constitution Article XXIV).
